item.title: Create .ipa files without a developer account
item.description: Archive the target Right-click the archive and click “Show in Finder” Right-click the .xcarchive file and click “Show Package Contents” Go into the Products folder Rename Applications to Payload Right-click the Payload folder and click “Compress …” Renamed the generated .

item.title: IP and BGP ASN Lookup Services
item.description: Web § Global § Hurricane Electric BGP Toolkit https://bgp.tools/ can discover recursive DNS (“local DNS”) resolver IPs. https://speed.cloudflare.com/ China (for users in Mainland China) § https://ping.

item.title: col
item.description: Convert Tabs to Spaces § ethtool -l eth0 | col -x converts tabs to spaces with proper indentation.

item.title: Grafana Dashboards
item.description: Issues Encountered § Broken Links § $__cell does not work in the latest Grafana table panel. Replace them with ${__value.text} instead. Dashboard Examples § https://github.

item.title: Asahi Linux
item.description: Features § https://github.com/AsahiLinux/docs/wiki/Feature-Support Speakers § Built-in speaker is supported on M1 and M2 MacBooks. GPU § Do not use apps from Flatpak on Asahi Linux.

item.title: Vertico
item.description: Key bindings in minibuffer § Preview: C-SPC for +vertico/embark-preview Next & previous history: M-p and M-n Page up & down: C-S-j and C-S-k (C-S- does not work in tty) Next & previous group: C-M-j and C-M-k Next & previous row: C-j and C-k Exit with input: M-RET (vertico-exit-input) or RET (exit-minibuffer) Search pattern § Consult Search in all key bindings § Usually SPC h b b, but use C-h b b or <doom-leader-alt-key> instead of SPC when leader key (SPC) is not available, for example in a vertico minibuffer: scroll-up-command <kp-next>, C-S-j vertico-scroll-up <remap> <scroll-up-command> Copy candidate list § C-; E or C-c C-; in minibuffer to export candidates to a new buffer with Embark.

item.title: Azure Accelerated Networking
item.description: Azure SmartNIC § Based on FPGAs, AccelNet offloads packet forwarding to SmartNIC hardware with Generic Flow Tables. GFT is a match-action language that defines specific operations on packets for one specific network flow.

item.title: Building an RPM package from source
item.description: sudo dnf install rpmdevtools rpmdev-setuptree cd ~/rpmbuild/SPECS vim pkg.spec # and save # Prepare build dependencies and sources sudo dnf builddep pkg.

item.title: Sideloading Blink with AltStore
item.description: Build from Source § Provisioning profile “iOS Team Provisioning Profile: …” doesn’t support the Associated Domains, Fonts, iCloud, and Push Notifications capability.

item.title: Fuzzing yash
item.description: AFL++ § Build Flags § cc_params[cc_par_cnt++] = "-g"; if (!have_o) cc_params[cc_par_cnt++] = "-O3"; Without -O flags, afl-cc will add -O3.
